# CircleCI CLI

Fetch the log output for a specific job number.

The project is inferred from the current git repository's remote
unless overridden with --project.

To infer the job from the latest run, use 'circleci logs'
with --last-failed or --last-job.

JSON fields: step, status, exit_code, output

## Usage

`circleci job logs <job-number> [flags]`

## Flags

| Flag               | Description                                             |
| ------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------- |
| `--jq string`      | Process values from the response using jq syntax        |
| `--json`           | Output as JSON                                          |
| `--project string` | Project slug (e.g. gh/org/repo); defaults to git remote |
| `--step string`    | Filter output to a single step by name                  |

## Inherited Flags

| Flag                  | Description                                                  |
| --------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `-c, --config string` | path to config file (default: ~/.config/circleci/config.yml) |
| `--debug`             | enable debug logging                                         |
| `-q, --quiet`         | suppress informational output; data on stdout is unaffected  |

## Examples

- Fetch logs for job number 123: 
  `circleci job logs 123`
- Filter to a specific step: 
  `circleci job logs 123 --step "Run tests"`
- Specify the project explicitly: 
  `circleci job logs 123 --project gh/myorg/myrepo`
- Output as JSON: 
  `circleci job logs 123 --json`

## Learn More

- Use `circleci <command> <subcommand> --help` for more information about a command.
- Read the manual at <https://circleci.com/docs/local-cli>
- Support at <https://github.com/CircleCI-Public/circleci-cli/issues>
